title: Northeastern University president’s compensation exceeds $3 million, records show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Northeastern University's President Joseph Aoun earned $3,045,428 in FY2025, making him the highest-paid college president in Massachusetts.
Aoun's compensation increased by 113% from FY21 to FY22, partly due to deferred compensation and bonus pay.
MIT President Sally Kornbluth was the second-highest paid, earning $2,151,788 for FY2025, nearly $1 million less than Aoun.

title: Cambridge man killed in early-morning shooting, investigators say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A man identified as Xavier Bautista, 32, was found dead from a gunshot wound in Cambridge around 5:30 a.m.
The shooting occurred near the intersection of Broadway and Norfolk Street, with police alerted by a pedestrian's 911 call.
Cambridge police and Middlesex District Attorney’s Office are investigating, seeking public information via 617-349-3300 or anonymous tips.

title: New England Aquarium trainer joins effort to save endangered penguin chicks in South Africa
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Diana Major from New England Aquarium volunteered in Cape Town to care for endangered penguin chicks.
African penguins could become functionally extinct by 2035 without significant conservation efforts.
The Aquarium supports field conservation and education to protect wild African penguin populations.

title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras apologizes for recent on-field confrontations: ‘It’s been a really tough week’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox's Willson Contreras is appealing a seven-game suspension for his involvement in a brawl against the Nationals.
Contreras dealt with multiple confrontations last week amid emotional strain from two devastating earthquakes in Venezuela.
Contreras and Nationals' Cade Cavalli each apologized for their roles in escalating tensions during last week's incident.

title: Red Sox Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected to MLB All-Star Game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitchers Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected for the 2026 MLB All-Star game.
Aroldis Chapman has a 2.10 ERA with 33 strikeouts and 17 saves in 25.2 innings this season.
Ranger Suarez, with a 2.94 ERA and 92 strikeouts in 88.2 innings, returns to Philadelphia for the All-Star game.

title: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown after trade from Celtics: ‘This is what keeps you hungry’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown after Celtics trade, citing lack of respect as a motivator for future success.
Brown was traded to the Philadelphia 76ers, feeling disrespected after being overlooked by Celtics management, notably Brad Stevens.
Trade speculation had persisted for months, with Pierce expressing disappointment but optimism about Boston's future with Paul George.

title: Photos: Boston celebrates the Fourth of July
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Boston kicked off Fourth of July with a parade and Declaration reading, celebrating the 250th anniversary of independence.
Mayor Michelle Wu and locals gathered at the Old State House for the Declaration of Independence reading.
The Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular featured performances and a drone show, marking the holiday's grand finale.

title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin leads the Red Sox in OPS (.977) and home runs (7) over the past 30 days.
Durbin's recent success follows poor early 2026 performance, now batting .321 since late May.
Boston Red Sox have team control over Caleb Durbin until 2031, providing future roster flexibility.

title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Aroldis Chapman set the MLB relief pitcher strikeout record with his 1,364th strikeout against the Angels.
Chapman achieved the milestone in 847 innings, surpassing Hoyt Wilhelm's record set over 1,872.1 innings.
The Red Sox celebrated Chapman's achievement with a video montage; he has struck out 118 batters since 2025.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston ranked as the most collision-prone city in the U.S., with an average of 3.7 years between crashes.
Worcester and Springfield, Massachusetts also ranked among the bottom five for collision frequency in the Allstate report.
Providence, Rhode Island placed 194th, making it one of the top 10 riskiest U.S. cities for drivers.
